name: Technical Writing
description: Developer documentation, tutorials, release notes, API references, and polished docs artifacts.

skills.rationale: Classifies docs work and writes clear tutorials, how-tos, README content, and explanations
Structures repo documentation with clear ownership, audience separation, and cross-links
Generates code-to-docs content, detects documentation drift, and applies repeatable templates
Builds architecture docs with project discovery, C4-style structure, and Mermaid diagrams
Creates, edits, redlines, and validates Word documents for polished deliverables
Extracts, reviews, and packages PDF content used in specs, reports, and documentation QA
Turns technical narratives into slide decks for launches, reviews, and stakeholder education
Converts DOCX, PDF, and Markdown in batches for docs migrations and publication workflows
Produces exhaustive research-grade project documentation with pedagogical depth
Documents and validates reusable agent skills with concise triggers and progressive disclosure
Turns implementation knowledge into readable tutorials and learning paths
Maintains agent-facing instructions such as CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md as living docs

agents.rationale: Reads unfamiliar codebases and extracts accurate concepts before drafting docs
Reviews docs for audience fit, accuracy, missing prerequisites, and stale examples

mcp.rationale: Reads issues, PRs, discussions, and release history while drafting docs and changelogs
Traverses local source, docs, examples, and generated artifacts during documentation passes
